briefing.opening: Good morning. I will now begin the climate briefing for April 14, 2026.

sections.title: 🇰🇷 Enhancing Domestic Climate Change Awareness and Expanding Education
sections.content: Today, various efforts to raise awareness and expand education on climate change were reported domestically. Anyang Greenmaru is operating a carbon-neutral practice sharing school for elementary students, actively promoting experiential education [1, 5, 7, 31], and Anyang City's Greenmaru is also conducting environmental classes through renewable energy experiences [8, 38]. Several local governments, including Chungcheongbuk-do, Cheongju City, and Busanjin-gu, are encouraging public interest and participation by hosting climate crisis photo exhibitions and citizen-participatory events as part of 'Climate Change Week' [2, 4, 6]. Furthermore, Seongnam City is focusing on strengthening expert capabilities by providing carbon neutrality and climate crisis response education for professionals in the environmental sector [21]. These efforts are interpreted as positive signals that enhance environmental sensitivity and encourage practical climate action across the country.

sections.title: 🌱 Accelerating Carbon Neutrality Efforts and Expanding Renewable Energy
sections.content: News of concrete actions to achieve carbon neutrality goals and expand renewable energy also continued. Pohang City is investing 3.1 billion won to accelerate the adoption of electric vehicles, thereby speeding up carbon neutrality [10, 11], and Uiwang Urban Corporation has signed a business agreement for future carbon neutrality [12]. Gwangju Metropolitan City is gaining attention for establishing the nation's first carbon-neutral model that produces tap water using solar power plants [22, 30], and the Seosan Biogasification Facility is emerging as a leading success story in the era of carbon neutrality [19]. In the renewable energy sector, Korea Western Power is promoting the nation's first public-led, community-participatory RE100 solar power project, and Korea Midland Power and Boryeong City have announced plans to install solar power facilities in all public parking lots [39, 41, 43]. These technological advancements and policy supports are expected to further accelerate energy transition and the shift towards a carbon-neutral society [34, 35, 46].

sections.title: ⚠️ Intensifying Climate Threats and the Urgency of Response
sections.content: However, there were also reports warning of the intensifying threats from climate change and the urgency of response. Canada's insurance industry issued a warning about 'climate risk' ahead of wildfire season [3], and in Philadelphia, USA, a record-breaking April heatwave is projected [51]. Domestically, an analysis indicated that the government's climate finance for achieving carbon neutrality goals is short by approximately 20 trillion won annually, with concerns raised that the budget allocation rate is falling below statutory plans [47]. Additionally, civic groups launched a petition demanding a halt to the construction of new gas power plants in an era of high oil prices and carbon neutrality [18]. Internationally, melting glaciers are sending a clear warning about climate change [63], and reports indicate that climate change is severely impacting California residents, with concerns that water conservation efforts are not keeping pace with the speed of climate change [52, 58]. This once again reminds us that the climate crisis is no longer a distant future problem but a serious, ongoing challenge.

sections.title: 🌍 International Community's Climate Response Discussions and Challenges
sections.content: The international community continues discussions on climate change response but faces complex challenges. Concerns about climate change in the U.S. are nearing their peak [50], and international interest is high, with former Secretaries of State discussing Iran and climate change [53]. Major international conferences are scheduled, such as Türkiye confirming the date for the COP31 summit and the imminent India Diaspora Climate Summit [69, 70], but India's withdrawal of its bid to host COP33 illustrates the complexity of international cooperation [74]. Meanwhile, an analysis suggests that renewable energy has surged due to the Middle East conflict, increasing China's influence, indicating the impact of energy transition on geopolitical dynamics [40, 44]. However, various perspectives and challenges for climate action still exist, as shown by Besant questioning the causes and economic costs of climate change [49], and criticisms that big tech companies have become quiet on climate change [65].

briefing.closing: As we have seen in today's briefing, efforts and awareness to address climate change are spreading both domestically and internationally, yet the threats are also intensifying. This is a time that demands continuous attention and action. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.description: Government: 'This year marks the first year of full-scale adoption… will become a new growth engine.' Domestic adoption faces challenges due to high installation costs and apartment-centric housing. ChatGPT-generated concept diagram of a heat pump. Provided by OpenAI. On the 3rd of last month, an amendment to the enforcement decree of the 'Act on the Promotion of the Development, Use, and Dissemination of New and Renewable Energy' (New and Renewable Energy Act) was approved by the State Council. The core of the amendment was to include 'air source energy' as renewable energy. Air source energy is related to heat pumps...

summary.phenomenon: On the 3rd of last month, an amendment to the enforcement decree of the 'Act on the Promotion of the Development, Use, and Dissemination of New and Renewable Energy' was approved by the State Council, with the core being the inclusion of air source energy as renewable energy.
summary.cause: The inclusion of air source energy as renewable energy is related to heat pumps and is considered a potential alternative amidst the energy crisis originating from the Middle East.
summary.outlook: The government anticipates this year to be the first year of full-scale heat pump adoption, expecting it to become a new growth engine. However, high installation costs and apartment-centric housing environments pose challenges to its widespread adoption in Korea.

articles.original_description: 정부 “올해 보급 본격화 원년…신성장 동력될 것”국내 보급은 높은 설치 비용, 아파트 위주 주거 난관챗GPT로 만든 히트펌프 개념도. 오픈AI 제공지난달 3일 ‘신에너지 및 재생에너지 개발·이용·보급 촉진법’(신재생에너지법) 시행령 개정안이 국무회의에서 의결됐다. 개정안의 핵심은 ‘공기열 에너지’를 재생에너지에 포함한다는 것이었다. 공기열 에너지는 히트펌프···

articles.title: [Exclusive] When Will Carbon Neutrality Be Achieved?… 'Climate Finance Shortfall of 20 Trillion Won Annually, Allocation Rate 74.2% of Target'
articles.original_title: [단독]이래서 언제 탄소중립하나···“기후 재정 연간 20조 부족, 목표 대비 편성률 74.2%”
articles.description: Members of the Climate Crisis Emergency Action are holding a press conference on August 21 last year at Gwanghwamun Square in Seoul, demanding a public audit of the government's carbon neutrality basic plan. Senior Reporter Seo Seong-il. It has been estimated that the 'climate budget' needed to support the government's 2050 carbon neutrality goal is approximately 20 trillion won short annually compared to the actual allocated amount. The budget allocation rate has also fallen year by year compared to the statutory plan, dropping below 80% last year...

summary.phenomenon: It has been estimated that the climate budget required for the government to achieve its 2050 carbon neutrality goal is approximately 20 trillion won short annually compared to the actual allocated amount. Furthermore, the budget allocation rate has consistently fallen below the statutory plan each year, dropping below 80% last year.
summary.outlook: Difficulties are anticipated in achieving the 2050 carbon neutrality goal due to insufficient climate finance and a low budget allocation rate.

articles.original_description: 기후위기비상행동 회원들이 지난해 8월21일 서울 광화문광장에서 ‘정부의 탄소중립기본계획에 대한 공익감사청구’ 기자회견을 하고 있다. 서성일 선임기자정부가 2050년 탄소중립 목표를 달성하려면 이를 뒷받침할 ‘기후 예산’은 실제 편성 규모보다 연간 약 20조원 부족한 것으로 추산됐다. 지금까지의 예산 편성률도 법정 계획 보다 해마다 떨어져며 지난해엔 80%를···

articles.description: Global demand rapidly increasing… BYD's sales in Vietnam up 153% month-over-month. Electric vehicles dominating the streets. VinFast electric vehicles from Vietnam are driving on the streets of Hanoi on the 14th. Due to rising oil prices influenced by the conflict between the U.S./Israel and Iran, demand for electric vehicles has increased, leading to a 127% year-over-year increase in VinFast's EV sales last month. EPA Yonhap News. The conflict between the U.S./Israel and Iran...

summary.phenomenon: Global demand for electric vehicles is rapidly increasing due to rising oil prices influenced by the conflict between the U.S./Israel and Iran. In Vietnam, VinFast's EV sales increased by 127% year-over-year last month, and BYD's sales increased by 153% month-over-month.
summary.cause: The primary cause of the increased demand for electric vehicles is the rise in oil prices stemming from the conflict between the U.S./Israel and Iran.

articles.title: Welcome to The Hotspot, our new newsletter on sport’s relationship with the climate crisis
articles.description: We delve into the best stories on how sport is changing around the climate crisis, and what can be done to navigate a way forwardTo subscribe to The Hotspot, just visit this pageNelson Mandela said: “Sport can create hope where once there was only despair.” Too optimistic? In 2026, almost certainly. Sport is still a common language, uniting unlikely groups like an all-powerful Esperanto, but it is in trouble.The pitches we play on, rivers we swim, seas we surf, mountains we climb, parks we run i

summary.phenomenon: Sport is noted to be undergoing transformation and facing challenges due to the climate crisis. It suggests that optimism regarding the positive role of sport may be overly ambitious by 2026, and implies that the environments where sporting activities occur, including pitches, rivers, seas, mountains, and parks, are being impacted.
summary.cause: The cause of sport's difficulties is explicitly stated to be the climate crisis.
summary.outlook: The article proposes the necessity of exploring forward paths to address the climate crisis challenges confronting sport. It highlights that the environments where sporting activities occur are being impacted, and underscores the need for developing solutions.
